issue: Revision: Replaced 'Then I'll hold my tongue' (subtext 5, on-the-nose acquiescence) with a wordless action beat — Bata eats millet — that performs acceptance without naming it (McKee Trialogue: the third thing is the bowl). Revision: Cut Panov's water-in-boots elaboration (lower-subtext padding). Compressed 'Good job it's dry. Last week...' to 'Dry, at least.' (same beat, tighter, raises subtext via what's omitted). Added Avdeev's deflective 'They say a lot of things' as Trialogue (the third thing = rumor itself, not the man). Final line lets Panov speak the name as a small confession of fear. Action line trimmed: removed adjectival hedging. | Reason: Subtext-gate fail (scene_avg 6.0). Tightened to fewer, denser lines. Voice-fidelity: Panov's working-class plain register holds; Avdeev's silent-watch

Revision: Avdeev's confession was the lowest-subtext line of the audit (3.0) — too explicit ('I feel so dull', 'living like a king'). Replaced with a half-sentence he cannot finish: action carries what words named. Panov's 'Well, brother' kept (the audit's only strong line, 7). Bata's pidgin tightened from full sentences to staccato — Panov's 'On your knees. Both of you' compressed to 'Knees.' (one word does the work of seven). Bata's letter line cut 'For prince' redundancy. Action lines tightened. Net shorter, denser. | Reason: Subtext-gate fail (scene_avg 5.4 — worst of the five). Avdeev's confession unfinished is the central change: the unfinishable sentence is the McKee Un

Revision: Avdeev's recognition-of-enemy speech was over-stated ('What a talk', 'Molly bar?'). Tightened to fragments: 'wife', 'two children', 'we talked' — the inability to phrase it fully IS the meaning. Cut his second confirmation line ('Still — fine fellows') which doubled the beat. Replaced with a single 'Maybe' that ends not in agreement and not in defiance. Bata's 'Prince want. Big business' cut to 'Prince. Business.' — same authority, half the words. Nikitin's threat preserved nearly verbatim — already at subtext-5 ceiling for that character archetype, his bluntness IS his voice. | Reason: Subtext-gate fail. Three of four lines reworked. Avdeev's enemy-as-human realization is the moral crux of the Act-2 picket sequence and now lands via
